leadership.title: Executive Message
leadership.message:
At Capital Union Investment, my approach is built on experience, precision, and a deep understanding of the Spanish property market.
With nearly two decades of hands-on experience, I have had the privilege of guiding international clients through property acquisitions, sales, and investment decisions with a strong focus on legal security and long-term value. Since beginning my career in 2008, I have developed a comprehensive expertise across all stages of the real estate process — from transaction management and legal coordination to new-build development analysis and investment strategy.
My background also includes leading short-term rental operations, providing a clear and practical understanding of real market performance, profitability, and asset optimization. This allows me to offer clients not just properties, but well-structured investment opportunities aligned with their goals.
My commitment is simple: to deliver a highly personalized, transparent, and results-driven service that ensures confidence, efficiency, and long-term success for every client.
leadership.name: Anna Alizarenko

hero.title: Privacy Policy
hero.subtitle: How we collect, use, and handle your data.

content.title: Privacy Policy
content.description: This Privacy Policy outlines how your data is managed across our platforms. We respect your privacy and are committed to protecting it through our compliance with this policy.

sections.title: 1. Information Collection
sections.text: We collect several different types of information for various purposes to provide and improve our Service to you. This includes personally identifiable information such as email address, first name and last name, phone number, and address.

sections.title: 2. How We Use Your Data
sections.text: Capital Union uses the collected data for various purposes: to provide and maintain our Service, to notify you about changes, to provide customer support, to gather analysis so that we can improve our Service, and to monitor the usage of our Service.

sections.title: 3. Data Security
sections.text: The security of your data is important to us, but remember that no method of transmission over the Internet, or method of electronic storage is 100% secure. While we strive to use commercially acceptable means to protect your Personal Data, we cannot guarantee its absolute security.

hero.title: Terms of Service
hero.subtitle: Rules and guidelines for using our platform.

content.title: Terms of Service
content.description: By accessing or using our platform, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you disagree with any part of the terms, then you may not access our services.

sections.title: 1. Intellectual Property
sections.text: The Service and its original content, features, and functionality are and will remain the exclusive property of Capital Union and its licensors. The Service is protected by copyright, trademark, and other laws of both the country and foreign countries.

sections.title: 2. User Obligations
sections.text: When you create an account with us, you must provide us information that is accurate, complete, and current at all times. Failure to do so constitutes a breach of the Terms, which may result in immediate termination of your account on our Service.

sections.title: 3. Limitation of Liability
sections.text: In no event shall Capital Union, nor its directors, employees, partners, agents, suppliers, or affiliates, be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential or punitive damages, including without limitation, loss of profits, data, use, goodwill, or other intangible losses.

hero.title: Cookie Policy
hero.subtitle: Information about our use of cookies.

content.title: Cookie Policy
content.description: Our site uses cookies to distinguish you from other users. This helps us to provide you with a good experience when you browse our website and also allows us to improve our site.

sections.title: 1. Types of Cookies We Use
sections.text: We use Session Cookies to operate our Service. We use Preference Cookies to remember your preferences and various settings. We use Security Cookies for security purposes. All of these ensure an optimal and seamless browsing experience.

sections.title: 2. Third-Party Cookies
sections.text: In addition to our own cookies, we may also use various third-parties cookies to report usage statistics of the Service, deliver advertisements on and through the Service, and so on. These providers have their own strict data policies.

sections.title: 3. Managing Your Cookies
sections.text: You can instruct your browser to refuse all cookies or to indicate when a cookie is being sent. However, if you do not accept cookies, you may not be able to use some portions of our Service. We recommend retaining them for full platform usage.

items.question: What is the process of buying a property in Spain?
items.answer: The process involves selecting a property, making a reservation, signing a private contract, and completing the purchase in the notary.

items.question: How long does it take to complete a property purchase?
items.answer: For a second-hand property purchase it takes between 4 to 8 weeks, depending on legal checks and financing. For a new build it can take from 1 month to 3 years, depending on the stage of construction when you buy.

items.question: Can I buy a property in Spain without being physically present?
items.answer: Yes, the purchase can be completed remotely through a power of attorney.

items.question: What documents are required to buy a property?
items.answer: You will need a passport and NIE number. You will also be required to provide proof of the source of funds to comply with legal and financial regulations.

items.question: Do I need a Spanish bank account to purchase property?
items.answer: Yes, a Spanish bank account is typically required to manage payments and ongoing expenses. If you already have an EU bank account, you can use it for maintenance costs, but opening a Spanish account is recommended for easier management.

items.question: What is an NIE number and how do I obtain it?
items.answer: The NIE is a foreign identification number required for any legal or financial transaction in Spain. It can be obtained at a Spanish consulate abroad or at a police station in Spain.

items.question: Can foreigners get a mortgage in Spain?
items.answer: Yes, non-residents can obtain a mortgage, usually financing 60–70% of the property value.

items.question: What are the main differences when buying as a non-resident?
items.answer: Non-residents must comply with additional administrative and financial requirements, but the process is straightforward with proper guidance.

items.question: Do I need a lawyer to buy property in Spain?
items.answer: A lawyer is highly recommended to ensure legal security and review all documentation.

items.question: Can I give power of attorney to complete the purchase remotely?
items.answer: Yes, a power of attorney allows a legal representative to complete the entire process on your behalf.

items.question: What additional costs should I expect when buying a property?
items.answer: Buyers should expect around 12–13% in additional costs, including taxes and legal fees.

items.question: How much deposit is required?
items.answer: A deposit of 10% is typically required at the private contract stage for resale properties. In new developments, the reservation deposit usually ranges from €6,000 to €25,000 depending on the final price.

items.question: What percentage of the property value can I finance?
items.answer: Most banks finance between 60% and 70% of the property value for non-residents.

items.question: What are the typical mortgage conditions in Spain?
items.answer: Mortgage conditions vary, but include fixed or variable rates and require proof of income and financial stability.

items.question: Are there any ongoing taxes after purchase?
items.answer: Yes, there are annual property taxes (IBI) and community maintenance costs to consider.

items.question: Is Costa Blanca a good place for real estate investment?
items.answer: Costa Blanca offers strong demand, lifestyle appeal, and stable long-term value, making it one of the most sought-after investment destinations in Spain.

items.question: What type of properties generate the best rental income?
items.answer: Apartments with 1–2 bedrooms in prime locations close to the beach or golf resorts tend to generate the strongest rental demand and returns.

items.question: What factors affect property profitability?
items.answer: Location, property type, demand, management quality, and local council regulations and restrictions all directly affect profitability.

items.question: Can you help with commercial or large-scale investments?
items.answer: Yes, advisory services include commercial assets, hotels, and large-scale investment projects.

items.question: What is the difference between new build and resale properties?
items.answer: New build properties are newly constructed, while resale properties have had previous owners. New builds often come with modern standards and developer warranties.

items.question: Are new build properties safer to buy?
items.answer: New builds offer modern standards and guarantees, but require proper evaluation of the developer, project stage, and legal protections.

items.question: What should I consider when buying off-plan?
items.answer: It is important to assess the developer's track record, construction timelines, legal guarantees, and bank guarantees that protect your deposit.

items.question: Do you check property documentation before purchase?
items.answer: Yes, all key documentation — including title deeds, planning licences, and encumbrances — is thoroughly reviewed before proceeding with the purchase.

items.question: What role does the notary play in Spain?
items.answer: The notary ensures the transaction is legally valid, witnesses the signing of the title deed, and facilitates registration of the property in your name.

items.question: What services do you provide beyond the purchase?
items.answer: A comprehensive, end-to-end real estate service is offered, covering property search, sales and investment advisory, market analysis, negotiation, legal coordination, NIE assistance, bank account setup, financing management, relocation support, and post-purchase property management.

items.question: Do you help with utilities, taxes, and administration?
items.answer: Yes, full assistance is provided with utilities connection, local taxes, and all administrative procedures following the purchase.

items.question: Can you assist with relocation and settling in Spain?
items.answer: Yes, comprehensive support is available for relocation, residency applications, and settling into your new life in Spain.

items.question: Do you offer property management after purchase?
items.answer: Yes, ongoing support and full property management services — including rental management — are available after purchase.
